name: Ashten Barber
title: A Financial Visionary Redefining Wealth Mindsets
story:
Ashten Barber: A Financial Visionary Redefining Wealth Mindsets

For more than a decade, Ashten Barber has been helping individuals and families navigate the complexities of personal finance, economic uncertainty, and long-term wealth building. As the founder of Eclipse Financial Advisors, she has built her career around a simple but powerful belief: while financial markets move in predictable cycles, every person's financial journey is unique.

Her mission extends beyond managing investments. She focuses on understanding people—their goals, challenges, experiences, and aspirations—before developing financial strategies that truly fit their lives.

A Human-Centered Approach to Finance

Unlike traditional financial advisors who focus primarily on numbers, Ashten believes that effective financial planning begins with understanding the individual behind the portfolio.

Whether she is working with a young professional taking their first steps toward financial independence, an entrepreneur building long-term wealth, or someone adjusting to a major life transition, her approach remains the same: personalized guidance rooted in empathy and understanding.

Finance is not just about money. It is about helping people create the life they want.

This people-first philosophy has become one of the defining characteristics of her practice and the reason many clients trust her during some of the most important moments of their lives.

Early Lessons That Shaped Her Financial Mindset

Ashten’s perspective on money was formed long before she entered the financial industry.

Growing up, she was taught that money should be viewed as a privilege and a tool rather than a source of stress. Her mother’s disciplined approach to managing credit played a significant role in shaping those beliefs.

One experience, in particular, left a lasting impression on her.

“I had my first child before I found out that you could actually carry a balance. That blew my mind.”

For Ashten, credit was never intended to become debt. Instead, it was a resource to be used responsibly and strategically. Those early lessons continue to influence the advice she gives clients today.

An Unexpected Journey into Finance

Interestingly, finance was not Ashten’s original career plan.

Her first passion was acting. She spent time pursuing opportunities on stage and exploring a creative path. However, over time, she realized that the lifestyle was not aligned with the future she envisioned for herself.

Everything changed when she completed her first tax return.

What many people consider a tedious task sparked genuine curiosity and excitement in her. She became fascinated by how financial systems worked and began exploring opportunities within the industry.

Initially, she considered accounting, but it did not take long to recognize that something was missing.

“I’m not a paper person. I’m a people person.”

That realization ultimately led her to financial advising—a profession that allowed her to combine analytical thinking with meaningful human connection.

Breaking Financial Myths

One of the most common myths Ashten encounters is the belief that financial advisors are only necessary for wealthy individuals.

She strongly disagrees.

According to Ashten, financial planning should begin long before significant wealth is accumulated. In fact, establishing good habits and creating a clear strategy early can have a far greater impact on long-term success.

Her philosophy is built around three core principles:

  • Start with a plan
  • Build trust and clarity
  • Think beyond the present generation

She also identifies two mistakes that repeatedly prevent people from reaching their financial goals:

  1. Allowing emotions to drive financial decisions
  2. Waiting too long to get started

Her advice is straightforward:

Be honest with yourself.

Honest self-assessment creates the foundation for better financial decisions and sustainable progress.

Balancing Entrepreneurship and Family

As an entrepreneur, Ashten understands the challenges that come with balancing professional ambitions and personal responsibilities.

While growing Eclipse Financial Advisors, she has remained committed to spending quality time with family and maintaining personal well-being. She believes success should never come at the expense of the people and experiences that matter most.

For aspiring entrepreneurs, her advice is simple yet powerful:

“Be 100% sold on what you do—passion fuels persistence.”

When challenges arise, genuine belief in your mission becomes the driving force that keeps you moving forward.

The Bigger Picture

At its core, Ashten Barber’s story is not just about finance.

It is a story of perseverance, adaptability, personal growth, and empowering others to build better futures. Her journey demonstrates how success often emerges from unexpected paths and how meaningful impact comes from understanding people as much as understanding numbers.

  • Perseverance
  • Adaptability
  • Financial Empowerment
  • Trusting Your Own Path

Today, she continues to help clients gain clarity, confidence, and control over their financial lives while encouraging them to define success on their own terms.

Her message remains timeless:

“Trust your intuition, block out external noise, and live life on your own terms.”

name: Ama Ramos Pipim
title: Blossoming Resilience: Ama’s Paper Flower Odyssey
story:
Blossoming Resilience: Ama’s Paper Flower Odyssey

Ama Ramos Pipim’s journey is a remarkable story of resilience, creativity, and self-discovery. As a Ghanaian-American paper flower artist and entrepreneur, her path to success was not a straight line but rather a journey shaped by personal challenges, growth, and an unwavering commitment to pursuing her passion.

Today, Ama is the creative force behind Wild Fleur Blooms, a brand rooted in love, artistry, and joy. However, her entrepreneurial journey began during one of the most difficult periods of her life.

Finding Light Through Creativity

After experiencing postpartum depression, Ama found herself searching for something that could reignite her sense of purpose and creativity. The turning point came while planning her daughter's birthday celebration.

Working within a limited budget, she began searching for affordable decoration ideas and discovered paper flowers on Pinterest. Captivated by their beauty but discouraged by their cost, she initially considered abandoning the idea.

Fortunately, her sister encouraged her to create the flowers herself.

That simple encouragement became the spark that changed everything.

With determination and curiosity, Ama began experimenting with paper flower crafting. What started as a practical solution quickly evolved into a creative outlet that brought healing, confidence, and joy.

The Birth of Wild Fleur Blooms

As Ama continued refining her skills, paper flower artistry transformed from a hobby into something much greater.

Over the years, she dedicated herself to learning new techniques, improving her craftsmanship, and developing her unique artistic style. Her passion eventually blossomed into Wild Fleur Blooms, a business built around creating beautiful handmade paper flowers that inspire happiness and celebration.

Every flower became a reflection of her journey—carefully crafted, resilient, and uniquely beautiful.

Turning Challenges into Opportunities

Like many entrepreneurs, Ama faced unexpected obstacles along the way.

When the COVID-19 pandemic disrupted plans and created uncertainty for small businesses worldwide, she was forced to adapt. Rather than allowing setbacks to define her future, she used the period as an opportunity for growth and innovation.

During this time, Ama explored new artistic techniques and developed a stronger focus on creating realistic paper flowers. She also embraced sustainable alternatives to fresh floral arrangements, giving clients long-lasting pieces that combined beauty with environmental consciousness.

The challenges of the pandemic ultimately became a catalyst for creative evolution.

Balancing Motherhood and Entrepreneurship

Building a business while raising children presented another set of challenges.

As a mother, Ama remained committed to ensuring that her daughters always felt loved, supported, and included in her journey. While entrepreneurship demanded long hours and dedication, family remained her highest priority.

She learned the importance of creating boundaries, protecting family time, and finding balance between professional ambitions and personal responsibilities.

That balance became one of the foundations of her success.

A Story of Resilience and Growth

Ama’s journey demonstrates that success is rarely linear. It is often shaped by moments of uncertainty, personal challenges, and unexpected opportunities.

Through perseverance and determination, she transformed a difficult chapter of her life into a thriving creative business that continues to inspire others.

Her story reflects the power of:

  • Resilience in the face of adversity
  • Creative self-expression
  • The importance of community support
  • Believing in your potential
  • Turning challenges into opportunities

The Bigger Picture

From navigating postpartum depression to becoming a successful entrepreneur, Ama Ramos Pipim’s story is a powerful reminder that growth often begins where comfort ends.

Through Wild Fleur Blooms, she has transformed paper into art, challenges into opportunities, and personal struggles into a source of inspiration for others.

Her journey proves that with passion, perseverance, and the courage to begin, even the most delicate creations can grow into something extraordinary.

name: Bonnie Christine
title: Bonnie Christine: A Journey from Mom to Million-Dollar Surface Pattern Designer
story:
Bonnie Christine: A Journey from Mom to Million-Dollar Surface Pattern Designer

In the vibrant world of surface pattern design, few stories are as inspiring as that of Bonnie Christine. A designer, educator, entrepreneur, and devoted mother, Bonnie has built a thriving creative business by transforming her passion for art into a global movement that empowers thousands of aspiring artists.

Known for her nature-inspired designs and signature floral patterns, Bonnie’s journey proves that success is not always about having the perfect background—it is often about having the courage to follow a dream and the consistency to pursue it every day.

Discovering a Hidden Calling

Bonnie’s path into design was anything but traditional.

After graduating from business school, she worked alongside her mother in a quilt store. Surrounded by colorful fabrics and beautiful patterns, she became fascinated by the artwork printed on the materials customers loved.

One realization changed everything:

Someone had designed every pattern she admired.

For the first time, Bonnie began to imagine herself as one of those designers.

Despite having no formal design education and little experience in the industry, the idea refused to leave her mind. Over the following months, she reflected on the possibility and slowly convinced herself that if others could build a career through design, she could too.

That belief became the foundation of her entrepreneurial journey.

Building a Dream One Day at a Time

With determination and a willingness to learn, Bonnie committed herself to becoming a surface pattern designer.

She focused on taking consistent daily actions that moved her closer to her goal. Rather than becoming overwhelmed by the size of the dream, she concentrated on steady progress and continuous improvement.

Her persistence paid off.

Just two years after beginning her journey, Bonnie achieved a major milestone when she signed a fabric licensing contract with Art Gallery Fabrics.

It was a defining moment that validated her hard work and proved that dedication could turn a dream into reality.

An Unexpected Opportunity to Teach

While Bonnie’s primary goal was to grow as a designer, life introduced an unexpected opportunity that would ultimately reshape her career.

Someone approached her with a simple request—to learn Adobe Illustrator.

Having become highly skilled in the software through her own design work, Bonnie agreed to teach what she knew.

What started as a small educational opportunity soon evolved into something much larger.

As she began teaching, Bonnie discovered that many aspiring creatives around the world were searching for guidance, confidence, and permission to pursue their artistic passions.

The stories of her students deeply resonated with her and revealed a greater purpose beyond creating designs.

Empowering a Global Community of Artists

Bonnie recognized a common challenge among aspiring artists: many doubted their own creative abilities or believed they needed permission before pursuing their dreams.

Determined to change that mindset, she made it her mission to help artists develop both their skills and confidence.

Her vision led to the creation of Immersion, a comprehensive surface pattern design course designed to teach artists not only the technical aspects of design but also how to build sustainable creative careers.

At the heart of the program was a philosophy that became central to Bonnie’s success:

Community over competition.

Rather than encouraging artists to compete against one another, she fostered an environment where creativity, collaboration, and mutual support could thrive.

The Joy Behind Surface Pattern Design

For Bonnie, surface pattern design is about much more than creating products.

It is about finding inspiration in everyday life and transforming it into something beautiful that enriches the world.

Whether designing fabrics, wallpapers, stationery, or home décor, she sees creativity as an opportunity to contribute beauty and joy to people's lives.

Her work is heavily inspired by nature, flowers, and the small details that often go unnoticed.

Through her designs, she encourages others to slow down, observe the world around them, and embrace their own creative expression.

From Creative Passion to Million-Dollar Success

Bonnie’s commitment to design, education, and community has resulted in more than personal fulfillment—it has helped her build a multimillion-dollar business while positively impacting artists across the globe.

Her success demonstrates that creativity and entrepreneurship are not mutually exclusive. With the right mindset, artists can create meaningful work while building sustainable and profitable careers.

More importantly, Bonnie has shown that success becomes even more rewarding when it is shared with others.

The Bigger Picture

Bonnie Christine’s story is about much more than surface pattern design.

It is a story of courage, consistency, and believing in possibilities before there is evidence that they exist. From a young mother working in a quilt store to a respected designer, educator, and entrepreneur, her journey serves as a powerful example of what can happen when passion is paired with persistence.

  • Following your creative calling
  • Taking consistent action toward your goals
  • Building community over competition
  • Empowering others through education
  • Turning passion into a thriving business

Today, Bonnie continues to inspire thousands of artists around the world, proving that creativity can be both meaningful and profitable.

Her journey reminds us that success is not reserved for those with perfect qualifications—it belongs to those willing to begin, learn, and keep moving forward.

name: Chelsea Espinoza
title: How Chelsea Espinoza Turned Healing Into Her Life’s Purpose
story:
How Chelsea Espinoza Turned Healing Into Her Life’s Purpose

For many women, the journey toward a better life rarely begins with a dramatic breakthrough. More often, it starts quietly—through exhaustion we normalize, dreams we postpone, or discomfort we learn to ignore.

For Chelsea Espinoza, that quiet discomfort eventually became impossible to overlook, setting her on a path that would transform not only her own life but the lives of countless women she would later serve.

Today, Chelsea is the founder of Road to Jubilee, a purpose-driven business dedicated to helping women create healthier, more meaningful, and fulfilling lives through coaching, leadership development, workshops, and speaking engagements. But before Road to Jubilee became a company, it began as something far more personal—a promise born from hardship, healing, and courage.

A Purpose Greater Than Business

When Chelsea talks about Road to Jubilee, she speaks about much more than entrepreneurship.

“Road to Jubilee is my company, but more than that, it’s my purpose. I help women improve their quality of life.”

For Chelsea, quality of life is not a trendy phrase or a vague aspiration. It is deeply personal and unique for every individual. For some, it means freedom. For others, peace, confidence, balance, or fulfillment.

Her mission is to help women reconnect with the parts of themselves that are often neglected while caring for everyone else.

That mission exists because she once lived that reality herself.

The Health Crisis That Changed Everything

For years, Chelsea devoted herself to work, relationships, responsibilities, and daily obligations while rarely making space for her own well-being.

Eventually, the cost became impossible to ignore.

Several years ago, she experienced severe and unexplained health issues that completely disrupted her life. Despite countless medical appointments, tests, and consultations, no clear answers emerged.

“I was sick, out of work for months, and uncertain if I would live. I knew I never wanted to be in that place again.”

What followed was not simply a recovery process—it was a profound personal transformation.

During her healing journey, Chelsea began asking difficult questions that many people avoid:

  • What truly matters?
  • What does a nourishing life look like?
  • How do you create a life you no longer need to escape from?

The answers slowly became the foundation for everything she would build in the future.

“This business is me living on purpose. It’s me taking action on the second chance I was given.”

The Vision Behind Road to Jubilee

Long before the business officially existed, the vision had already begun taking shape.

Chelsea spent years imagining a retreat space centered on restoration, connection, healing, and community—especially for women.

Every time she shared the concept, she noticed a similar reaction. Women immediately connected with the idea of having a place where they could simply pause and breathe.

She envisioned:

  • Beautiful gardens
  • Fresh food harvested by hand
  • Meaningful conversations
  • Shared meals and connection
  • A space for rest, reflection, and renewal
“The retreat property will be called Jubilee. I don’t know when or where it will exist yet, but I know the path I’m on leads there.”

That vision continues to guide her work today.

The Courage to Start Again

After regaining her health, Chelsea returned to her role as Vice President of a landscape construction company. Yet another realization soon emerged.

Although she had recovered physically, she still felt called toward something greater.

During a quiet vacation away from daily pressures, she experienced something she had not felt in a long time: complete clarity.

“No anxiety. No hesitation. I knew that I knew that I knew. It was time.”

While entrepreneurship was not entirely new territory—having previously worked as both an interior designer and business owner—building a mission-driven company presented a very different challenge.

Before launching, she intentionally gave herself space to reflect.

“I gave myself a month to decompress. To listen. To ask myself: What am I building? Who am I building this for?”

That period of reflection became essential to creating a business aligned with her purpose.

The Challenge of Building Trust

Starting over in a completely different industry was both exciting and humbling.

While friends, colleagues, and loved ones immediately supported her vision, building trust with clients required patience and persistence.

“What surprised me most was how long trust-building takes. This work is personal. It’s delicate. People have to feel safe with you.”

Because her work centers on personal growth, healing, and transformation, creating genuine trust became one of the most important aspects of her business.

Rather than viewing uncertainty as a sign to stop, Chelsea learned to embrace it as evidence of growth.

“There’s an uncomfortable growth spurt when you choose a new path. You question everything—am I doing the right thing? Am I enough? But those questions aren’t a stop sign. They’re part of the journey.”

Transforming Lives One Woman at a Time

When asked about the most rewarding part of her work, Chelsea finds it difficult to choose a single success story.

“Every woman I meet reminds me why I do this. Every single day.”

Many of her clients arrive during major life transitions—navigating burnout, career shifts, identity changes, or reconnecting with dreams they once abandoned.

One story, however, remains especially memorable.

A woman who had always dreamed of living in a particular place never believed that dream could become reality. Through intentional work and personal growth, she eventually stepped into the life she had once considered impossible.

“She did all the work. But watching her step into the life she thought would never happen was incredible.”

Moments like these continue to reinforce Chelsea’s purpose and passion.

The Bigger Picture

Chelsea Espinoza’s journey is ultimately about reclaiming ownership—over health, purpose, identity, and possibility.

Through Road to Jubilee, she continues helping women rediscover something many have forgotten: creating a meaningful life is not selfish. In many cases, it is the most important work a person can do.

  • Prioritizing personal well-being
  • Finding purpose through adversity
  • Embracing personal transformation
  • Creating space for healing and growth
  • Building a life aligned with your values

What began as a personal healing journey has evolved into a mission that empowers women to pursue lives filled with clarity, confidence, and intention.

Her story is a powerful reminder that sometimes the hardest seasons of life become the foundation for our greatest purpose.

name: Diana Edwards
title: Inner Compass with Life’s Purpose: The Journey of Diana Edwards
story:
Inner Compass with Life’s Purpose: The Journey of Diana Edwards

In Denver, Colorado, where snowy spring mornings often give way to bright afternoons, Diana Edwards has built a life defined by adaptability, purpose, and a willingness to follow her evolving passions. Her journey spans engineering, healthcare, entrepreneurship, and personal growth, illustrating how success often emerges when we align our careers with our deepest values and interests.

Today, Diana is known not only as a dedicated physical therapist but also as an entrepreneur and creative maker. Her story is one of transformation, resilience, and the courage to embrace unexpected opportunities.

Following a Path That Was Never Meant to Be Permanent

Diana grew up in a small town where scientific achievement and technical careers were highly valued. Encouraged by family expectations and her natural aptitude for mathematics and problem-solving, she pursued higher education in engineering.

Her academic journey led her to earn degrees in both Mechanical Engineering and Biomedical Engineering, preparing her for a career rooted in innovation and analytical thinking.

Yet despite her success, something felt incomplete.

While engineering challenged her intellectually, her interests often gravitated toward wellness, movement, and understanding the human body. The more she explored these passions, the clearer it became that her future might lie somewhere different.

Sometimes the path we begin on is not the path we are ultimately meant to follow.

Discovering a Passion for Healing

Outside of academics and professional pursuits, Diana found joy in activities that connected her to physical health and personal well-being.

Running, weightlifting, and studying human performance sparked a curiosity that grew stronger over time. She became increasingly fascinated by how the body functions, heals, and adapts.

Eventually, that curiosity evolved into a calling.

Rather than viewing her transition as a career change, Diana saw it as an opportunity to align her work with her purpose. She decided to pursue physical therapy, combining her scientific background with a genuine desire to improve people's lives.

The shift represented more than a new profession—it marked the beginning of a journey centered on healing, connection, and service.

A Holistic Approach to Wellness

As a physical therapist, Diana believes that true health extends far beyond treating physical symptoms.

Her approach focuses on the interconnected relationship between the body, mind, and lifestyle. Rather than viewing wellness through a narrow lens, she encourages a comprehensive perspective that includes:

  • Physical movement and rehabilitation
  • Nutrition and healthy habits
  • Quality sleep and recovery
  • Stress management and emotional resilience
  • Long-term lifestyle sustainability

Her commitment to holistic care is deeply personal.

Having experienced anxiety and chronic pain herself, Diana understands firsthand how physical and emotional well-being influence one another. Those experiences strengthened her empathy and enhanced her ability to connect with patients facing similar challenges.

Healing becomes more meaningful when it is guided by both professional expertise and personal understanding.

Finding Creativity Beyond Healthcare

While healthcare became a significant part of her professional identity, Diana's creativity found expression in an entirely different way.

During the pandemic, she began sewing as a creative outlet. What started as a relaxing hobby quickly developed into a passion for craftsmanship and design.

As her skills grew, she launched an Etsy business called Diana’s Sewing Creations, offering handmade products including:

  • Oven mitts
  • Skillet handle covers
  • Aprons
  • Custom sewn accessories

Each product reflected her commitment to quality, attention to detail, and love for creating something meaningful with her hands.

What began as a personal pastime evolved into a successful entrepreneurial venture, demonstrating that opportunities often emerge from the activities we enjoy most.

Lessons from Entrepreneurship

Building a business introduced Diana to a new set of challenges and rewards.

Like many entrepreneurs, she experienced moments of uncertainty, learning that success rarely happens overnight. Through every stage of growth, she embraced the importance of patience, consistency, and adaptability.

Rather than measuring success solely through revenue or sales, Diana focuses on creating work that feels purposeful and aligned with her values.

Entrepreneurship taught her that fulfillment comes not only from achievement but from enjoying the process of building something meaningful.

Passion creates momentum, but perseverance transforms passion into long-term success.

Guided by Core Values

Throughout every chapter of her journey—from engineering student to healthcare professional and entrepreneur—Diana has remained grounded in a clear set of personal values.

These principles serve as her compass when navigating uncertainty and making important decisions.

  • Family and meaningful relationships
  • Authenticity and personal integrity
  • Continuous learning and growth
  • Self-care and well-being
  • Purpose-driven work

These values help her stay aligned with what matters most, regardless of how her career continues to evolve.

The Bigger Picture

Diana Edwards' story is a powerful reminder that life rarely follows a perfectly straight line. Sometimes the most rewarding opportunities emerge when we are willing to listen to our inner voice and embrace change.

From engineering and physical therapy to entrepreneurship and creative expression, Diana has repeatedly demonstrated the courage to follow her interests and redefine success on her own terms.

Her journey highlights the importance of:

  • Trusting your evolving passions
  • Embracing career transitions
  • Prioritizing holistic well-being
  • Finding creativity in unexpected places
  • Building a life aligned with your values

As she continues exploring new opportunities and helping others improve their health and well-being, Diana remains a shining example of resilience, authenticity, and lifelong growth.

Her story reminds us that when we follow our inner compass, every twist and turn can become part of a meaningful and fulfilling journey.

name: Jan Miller
title: Designing What Lasts
story:
Designing What Lasts

For Jan Miller, design has never been just a profession. It is a way of thinking, a commitment to craftsmanship, and a lifelong pursuit of creating work that stands the test of time.

As the founder of 21-13 Impact Graphics, Jan has spent more than two decades navigating an industry defined by constant change. From the era of print and DVD packaging to today's world of streaming platforms, Canva templates, and artificial intelligence, he has remained relevant by embracing evolution without abandoning the fundamentals that make great design work.

His story is one of adaptability, resilience, and an unwavering dedication to building relationships that last.

Growing Up Around Design and Print

Long before launching his own company, Jan was immersed in the world of print production.

His father operated Royal Paper Box for decades, exposing him to conversations about paper stocks, printing techniques, finishes, packaging, and production processes from an early age.

These experiences gave him an understanding that many modern designers never fully develop.

What works on a screen does not always work in print—and understanding that difference matters.

That foundation would later become one of his greatest professional advantages.

From DVD Production to Brand Building

Jan's early career began during the peak of the DVD and Blu-ray era.

Working for a DVD production company, his original responsibilities centered around video editing and programming. Over time, however, clients began requesting additional creative services.

They needed:

  • DVD packaging design
  • Marketing materials
  • Posters and promotional graphics
  • Branding assets

Jan naturally stepped into those projects.

Before long, he found himself spending less time editing videos and more time helping businesses establish visual identities.

That shift would ultimately shape the future of his career.

The Birth of 21-13 Impact Graphics

Like many entrepreneurial stories, the launch of 21-13 Impact Graphics emerged from a moment of uncertainty.

As financial problems within his employer's company became increasingly apparent, Jan could see warning signs others ignored. Invoices remained unpaid, operations slowed, and the future looked uncertain.

When he eventually resigned, management asked him not to inform clients.

It turned out he did not need to.

The clients already knew who they trusted.

Within days, major customers and vendors began reaching out directly, choosing relationships over contracts.

Those relationships became the foundation upon which 21-13 Impact Graphics was built in 2005.

From the beginning, the business was driven not by marketing hype but by trust, reliability, and proven results.

Surviving the 2008 Financial Crisis

Every business eventually faces a defining test, and for Jan, that moment arrived during the 2008 financial crisis.

The independent film market contracted dramatically. Studio budgets tightened, physical media sales declined, and major industry buyers reduced spending.

Instead of panicking, Jan adapted.

He expanded his services beyond entertainment and diversified into:

  • Corporate branding
  • Logo design
  • Website development
  • Marketing campaigns
  • Promotional materials
  • Business identity systems

While continuing to serve entertainment clients, he successfully positioned the company to thrive in multiple industries.

Today, a significant portion of his work still supports entertainment companies—only now the assets are designed for streaming platforms rather than DVD shelves.

Adaptability became the company's greatest competitive advantage.

Thriving in an Industry That Never Stops Changing

Jan often compares graphic design to medicine.

In both professions, continuous learning is essential.

Software evolves, technologies change, and workflows that once required hours can become obsolete overnight.

Rather than resisting new tools, Jan has always chosen to understand them.

When Canva disrupted the design industry, he embraced it as a practical solution for simple projects while recognizing its limitations.

Businesses often overlook what they sacrifice when relying entirely on templates:

  • Print accuracy
  • Production expertise
  • Scalability
  • Ownership control
  • Professional file management

Then came artificial intelligence.

Like many industry professionals, Jan witnessed widespread concerns about automation replacing creative work. Yet his perspective remained measured.

“The goal isn’t automation. It’s amplification.”

For him, AI is a tool—not a replacement for expertise.

While AI can generate ideas and imagery, professionals still provide the judgment, refinement, production knowledge, and strategic thinking required to deliver meaningful results.

Building a Business Through Relationships

Jan believes that talent alone is rarely enough to sustain a business.

Relationships remain one of the most important drivers of long-term success.

His advice to entrepreneurs is straightforward: networking is not optional.

He recommends maintaining:

  • One closed networking group
  • One open networking group
  • One social networking community

Beyond that, he warns, many professionals spread themselves too thin.

Most importantly, networking requires consistent follow-up.

“The work exists. But it doesn’t come to you. You go get it.”

According to Jan, businesses rarely grow passively. Growth happens through intentional outreach, relationship-building, and consistent visibility.

Why Consistency Matters More Than Perfection

After observing countless businesses succeed and fail, Jan has developed a practical philosophy about entrepreneurship.

Award-winning businesses are rarely built through perfection.

Instead, they are built through:

  • Consistency
  • Patience
  • Listening carefully
  • Making smart decisions
  • Focusing on long-term relationships

He also understands the importance of recognizing personal limitations.

Rather than trying to do everything himself, Jan relies on specialists when needed, working alongside developers, financial professionals, and industry experts.

For him, strong businesses are built through collaboration and trust.

A New Venture: The Glass Leash

One of Jan’s most exciting recent projects combines technology, creativity, and emotional storytelling.

The Glass Leash began with portraits of his own dog and evolved into a specialized business that transforms everyday pet photographs into premium glass-mounted artwork.

Each image undergoes a detailed enhancement process that may include:

  • Background cleanup
  • Lighting adjustments
  • Image restoration
  • Detail enhancement
  • Artistic refinement

The final artwork is printed directly onto glass, creating vibrant, frameless pieces designed to preserve cherished memories.

In addition to pet portraits, Jan also creates memorial pieces that celebrate the emotional connection people share with loved ones and animals.

It is more than photography—it is storytelling preserved in glass.

The Bigger Picture

After more than twenty years in design, Jan Miller has remained successful not because he resisted change, but because he welcomed it.

His journey demonstrates that long-term success comes from balancing creativity with adaptability, expertise with curiosity, and innovation with timeless fundamentals.

Through every shift in technology and every economic cycle, he has continued building businesses, serving clients, and creating meaningful work by staying committed to learning and growth.

  • Embrace change instead of fearing it
  • Build relationships before you need them
  • Master the fundamentals
  • Stay curious and keep learning
  • Create value that lasts
“Research. Take notes. Learn from companies doing it well. Network online and in person. You have to reach out. You have to talk to people. That’s how businesses are built.”

In an industry defined by constant evolution, Jan Miller's story is a reminder that curiosity, adaptability, and craftsmanship never go out of style.

name: Karen Brown
title: Karen Brown’s Road to Mastery with ‘Just Get It Done Quilts’
story:
Karen Brown’s Road to Mastery with ‘Just Get It Done Quilts’

Success rarely happens overnight. More often, it is built through years of dedication, countless lessons, and the willingness to keep moving forward even when progress feels slow. Few stories demonstrate this better than that of Karen Brown, the creator behind the widely recognized quilting platform Just Get It Done Quilts.

What began as a passion for quilting eventually evolved into a thriving online community, educational platform, and influential YouTube channel. Through persistence, creativity, and an unwavering commitment to serving others, Karen has become a respected voice in both the quilting and content creation worlds.

Balancing Family, Business, and Creativity

Before launching Just Get It Done Quilts, Karen already had a full life.

Together with her husband, she operated a successful appliance business while raising four children. Managing both entrepreneurship and family responsibilities demanded significant time, energy, and discipline.

Yet throughout those busy years, her passion for quilting remained a constant source of joy and creativity.

Eventually, that passion inspired her to explore a new opportunity: sharing her knowledge online through YouTube.

What followed was the beginning of an entirely new chapter.

Every success story is built on hundreds of small decisions, lessons, and improvements made over time.

Learning the Art of Content Creation

Entering the world of YouTube was far more challenging than Karen initially expected.

Creating videos required an entirely new set of skills, including:

  • Filming and camera work
  • Video editing
  • Content planning
  • Script writing
  • Audience engagement
  • Technical production

Like many first-time creators, Karen encountered a steep learning curve. The early stages were filled with trial and error, experimentation, and continuous learning.

Rather than becoming discouraged, she embraced the process and focused on improving with every video she produced.

Over time, those small improvements accumulated into expertise.

The Work Behind Every Video

To viewers, a polished YouTube video may seem effortless. Behind the scenes, however, each piece of content represents hours of preparation and dedication.

Karen openly shares the amount of work required to maintain a high-quality content platform.

Each video often involves:

  • Research and planning
  • Project preparation
  • Filming multiple segments
  • Writing scripts and outlines
  • Editing and post-production
  • Creating newsletters and supporting content

Her commitment to quality has become one of the defining characteristics of her brand and a key reason audiences continue returning to her content.

Consistency is important, but quality is what keeps people coming back.

Starting with What You Have

One of the most encouraging lessons Karen shares with aspiring creators is that expensive equipment is not required to get started.

Many people delay pursuing their goals because they believe they need professional cameras, advanced software, or expensive setups before taking action.

Karen challenges that assumption.

According to her, a smartphone is often more than enough to begin creating content and developing valuable skills.

What matters most is starting, learning, and improving over time.

She also reminds creators that immediate success is rare. Building an audience requires patience, persistence, and a willingness to continue showing up even when growth feels slow.

Authenticity in a Commercial World

As her audience expanded, opportunities for sponsorships and product collaborations naturally followed.

While these partnerships can create new revenue streams, Karen believes maintaining authenticity is essential.

Rather than promoting products solely for financial gain, she focuses on working with companies and products that align with her values and genuinely benefit her audience.

This approach allows her to preserve the trust she has spent years building.

Authenticity is one of the most valuable assets a creator can have.

The Influence of Family and Entrepreneurship

Karen’s entrepreneurial mindset did not emerge in isolation.

She credits much of her resilience and business perspective to the examples set by her family.

Inspired by her grandmother’s entrepreneurial spirit and her mother’s diverse ventures, Karen developed a deep appreciation for hard work, adaptability, and self-reliance.

These influences continue to shape the way she approaches both business and life.

Redefining Financial Freedom

For Karen, financial success is not simply about income.

True financial freedom means having the ability to pursue meaningful work, support her community, and focus on projects that bring fulfillment.

She believes one of the keys to building a successful online platform is identifying a clear niche and serving that audience consistently.

By focusing on quilting—a subject she genuinely loves—Karen was able to build a loyal community of viewers who share her enthusiasm and values.

Her journey demonstrates the remarkable power of digital platforms to amplify knowledge, creativity, and connection.

Building a Positive Community

As online audiences grow, creators inevitably encounter both encouragement and criticism.

Karen approaches feedback with thoughtfulness and balance.

She values constructive conversations that help improve her work while also recognizing the importance of establishing healthy boundaries.

Creating a welcoming and supportive environment remains one of her highest priorities.

Her community thrives because it is built on respect, encouragement, and a shared passion for creativity.

A strong community grows when people feel valued, heard, and supported.

Adapting in a Changing Digital Landscape

Social media platforms and audience behaviors continue to evolve rapidly.

Rather than resisting change, Karen encourages creators to remain flexible and open to experimentation.

Success in the digital world often comes from finding a unique voice and embracing opportunities for innovation while staying true to your core values.

For Karen, authenticity has always been more important than chasing trends.

The Bigger Picture

Karen Brown’s story is about much more than quilting.

It is a story of perseverance, lifelong learning, entrepreneurship, and community building. From balancing family responsibilities and running a business to becoming a respected content creator and educator, her journey illustrates what is possible when passion is paired with consistency.

Her experience highlights the importance of:

  • Starting before you feel fully ready
  • Embracing continuous learning
  • Prioritizing quality over shortcuts
  • Building authentic relationships
  • Creating value through service and education

Through Just Get It Done Quilts, Karen continues to inspire thousands of creators, makers, and entrepreneurs around the world.

Her journey reminds us that mastery is not achieved through perfection—it is earned through persistence, curiosity, and the courage to keep creating.

name: Karnam Malleswari
title: Triumph Over Adversity
story:
Triumph Over Adversity

Some stories transcend sport and become symbols of determination, resilience, and national pride. The journey of Karnam Malleswari is one such story—a remarkable tale of a young girl from a modest family who refused to let limitations, criticism, or circumstances define her future.

Through unwavering dedication and extraordinary perseverance, Karnam Malleswari rose from humble beginnings to become one of India’s most celebrated athletes, inspiring generations with her courage and commitment to excellence.

Humble Beginnings in Andhra Pradesh

Born and raised in a small town in Andhra Pradesh, Karnam Malleswari grew up in a family where hard work was a way of life.

Her father worked as a constable with Indian Railways, while her mother devoted herself to caring for the family. As one of five siblings, Malleswari was raised with strong values of discipline, perseverance, and determination.

Her introduction to weightlifting came unexpectedly through her sister, who had joined a local training center. Accompanying her sister to practice sessions, young Karnam became fascinated by the sport and the dedication of the athletes she watched every day.

What began as curiosity would soon evolve into a life-changing passion.

The Rejection That Sparked a Dream

Inspired by what she observed, Karnam approached a coach and expressed her desire to become a weightlifter.

The response was discouraging.

Looking at the young twelve-year-old girl, the coach dismissed her aspirations and suggested she was not suited for weightlifting. Instead, he believed she would be better off helping her mother with household responsibilities.

Those words could have ended her dream before it even began.

Instead, they ignited something powerful within her.

Rather than accepting someone else's judgment, Karnam chose to believe in herself. The rejection became fuel for her determination, strengthening her resolve to prove that potential cannot be measured by appearances alone.

Training Against the Odds

Without access to proper facilities or support, Karnam found alternative ways to pursue her goal.

Near her home stood an abandoned gym where she began training using whatever equipment was available. Often practicing barefoot and working with limited resources, she developed her skills through discipline and persistence.

Every training session reinforced a lesson that would define her career:

Success belongs to those who continue working when circumstances are far from ideal.

Her determination remained unshaken, even when resources were scarce and opportunities seemed distant.

A Turning Point

As her sister progressed to the national level, Karnam gained valuable exposure to competitive weightlifting.

During the selection trials for the 1990 Asian Games, she closely observed athletes from around the world, paying particular attention to the techniques and discipline of Russian competitors.

Her eagerness to learn did not go unnoticed.

Indian team coach Genedy Rebecon recognized her commitment, talent, and hunger for improvement. Although Karnam lacked formal qualifications at the time, he saw immense potential in her and decided to mentor her.

This opportunity became a defining moment in her journey.

Breaking Records and Winning Gold

Under expert guidance and through relentless training, Karnam's progress accelerated rapidly.

Within a year, she began breaking national records and winning gold medals at major competitions. Her success established her as one of India's most promising weightlifting talents.

Yet her ambitions extended far beyond national recognition.

She set her sights on the international stage and focused intensely on preparing for the 1995 World Weightlifting Championships in China.

For Karnam, the goal was about more than personal achievement.

She dreamed of hearing the Indian National Anthem played in her honor as a world champion.

That dream fueled every training session, every sacrifice, and every challenge she encountered along the way.

A Historic Moment for India

At the 1995 World Weightlifting Championships, Karnam delivered a performance that would secure her place in sporting history.

Competing against some of the world's strongest athletes, she demonstrated exceptional strength, focus, and determination.

The result was extraordinary:

  • Three Gold Medals
  • A World Record Performance
  • Global Recognition for Indian Weightlifting

Her achievement represented not only personal success but also a proud moment for India on the international stage.

The Anthem That Never Played

Ironically, the moment Karnam had envisioned for years unfolded differently than expected.

Although she had achieved her dream of becoming a world champion, a surprising administrative oversight prevented the Indian National Anthem from being played during the medal ceremony.

The official recording had not been submitted to the organizing committee.

For many athletes, such a moment might have felt disappointing.

Karnam responded differently.

Standing proudly on the podium with the Indian flag in her hands and her gold medal around her neck, she began singing the National Anthem herself.

In that unforgettable moment, determination, patriotism, and pride became more powerful than any recording.

The image of Karnam Malleswari singing for her country remains one of the most inspiring moments in Indian sporting history.

The Bigger Picture

Karnam Malleswari's journey is about far more than medals and records.

It is a story of believing in oneself when others do not, embracing challenges as opportunities, and pursuing excellence regardless of circumstances.

Her legacy continues to inspire athletes, entrepreneurs, and dreamers across generations.

  • Turning rejection into motivation
  • Persevering through adversity
  • Trusting your own potential
  • Representing your country with pride
  • Achieving greatness through determination

From a small town in Andhra Pradesh to the world stage, Karnam Malleswari proved that extraordinary achievements often begin with ordinary people who refuse to give up.

Her story reminds us that true champions are not defined by the obstacles they face, but by the courage with which they overcome them.

name: Kevin O’Leary
title: Business Tips from the Shark
story:
Business Tips from the Shark

Few entrepreneurs have built a reputation as recognizable as Kevin O’Leary. Known worldwide as an investor, businessman, and television personality, O’Leary has spent decades building companies, evaluating opportunities, and mentoring entrepreneurs.

While many know him from television, his success was built long before the cameras arrived. Through multiple ventures, major acquisitions, and billion-dollar exits, O’Leary developed a philosophy centered on discipline, focus, execution, and the relentless pursuit of personal freedom.

His journey offers valuable lessons for entrepreneurs, investors, and anyone seeking to create lasting success.

The Power of Focus

One of the most influential lessons Kevin learned came from his mentor, Jerry Patterson.

“If things go well, a lot of things will be thrown at you. You’re going to hear noise, criticism, and distractions from every direction. Successful managers learn how to focus on the goal and shut out the noise.”

That advice continues to guide O’Leary's daily routine.

Despite managing multiple businesses and investments, he focuses on three critical objectives each day. Rather than becoming overwhelmed by countless demands, he concentrates his energy on the tasks that create the greatest impact.

According to O’Leary, successful leaders learn to distinguish between:

  • Real opportunities
  • Unnecessary distractions
  • Urgent problems
  • Meaningful priorities

Focus, not activity, drives results.

A Commitment to Excellence

Throughout his career, O’Leary has maintained exceptionally high standards.

Even today, despite leading multiple businesses, he remains actively involved in creative and production work. Having started his career as a cameraman, editor, and producer, he continues to review commercials, marketing campaigns, and video projects personally.

If he notices a flaw, whether it is a jump cut, poor lighting, or a weak visual element, he wants it fixed.

Good enough is rarely good enough when excellence is the goal.

This commitment to quality has become one of the defining characteristics of his business philosophy.

The Discipline Behind Success

O’Leary believes success begins long before the workday starts.

His daily routine reflects the discipline he applies across every aspect of life:

  • Wake up at 5:00 AM
  • Exercise immediately after sunrise
  • Complete a cycling workout
  • Spend time in the gym
  • Begin work with a clear plan for the day

For him, physical discipline supports mental discipline.

More importantly, it reinforces the habits required to perform consistently over decades rather than weeks.

Why Prioritization Is a Leadership Skill

According to O’Leary, one of the hardest responsibilities of any entrepreneur is deciding what deserves attention.

Many leaders waste valuable time attempting to satisfy everyone.

He believes that approach inevitably leads to failure.

Managers who try to make everyone happy often accomplish very little.

Instead, effective leadership requires making difficult decisions, setting priorities, and focusing resources where they create the greatest value.

The ability to identify what truly matters separates successful leaders from overwhelmed managers.

The First Million Is the Hardest

O’Leary openly acknowledges that building wealth is difficult.

He remembers being told that earning his first million dollars would be incredibly challenging.

That prediction proved true.

Then people told him making five million dollars would be even harder.

They were right about that too.

But something changed along the way.

After years of experimentation, successes, and failures, he began to understand what worked and what didn't.

The early stages of success are often the hardest because experience has not yet caught up with ambition.

As knowledge and confidence grow, opportunities become easier to recognize and execute.

The Real Goal Is Freedom

Despite his financial success, O’Leary insists that money itself is not the ultimate objective.

For him, entrepreneurship is about something much more valuable: freedom.

“The whole reason for becoming an entrepreneur is not the greed of money. It's the pursuit of personal freedom.”

Today, he considers time his most valuable asset.

Rather than measuring wealth solely by dollars, he measures success by the ability to control how his time is spent.

To maximize productivity, he organizes each day into 30-minute blocks and carefully reviews upcoming priorities.

That structure allows him to make intentional decisions about where his attention goes.

The Importance of Financial Security

One of O’Leary’s most frequently shared financial principles involves protecting wealth once it has been created.

He advises entrepreneurs to reach a point where they have sufficient capital invested safely enough to support themselves indefinitely.

His reasoning is simple:

  • Financial security reduces fear
  • Fear often limits decision-making
  • Confidence creates better opportunities
  • Security allows calculated risk-taking

Once a solid financial foundation exists, entrepreneurs can pursue larger opportunities without jeopardizing everything they have built.

The Journey Before the Success

Many people view Kevin O’Leary as an overnight success.

The reality is very different.

His original dream was not business at all—it was filmmaking.

He wanted to become a photographer, cinematographer, and creative storyteller.

His father encouraged him to pursue education first, advising him to complete university and earn an MBA before chasing entrepreneurial ambitions.

That advice proved invaluable.

Armed with business knowledge and practical skills, O’Leary launched his first company producing sports content and television programming.

The business eventually attracted acquisition interest, providing him with his first major entrepreneurial success and teaching him how company-building truly worked.

Every successful entrepreneur begins by learning how value is created and why others are willing to pay for it.

Building The Learning Company

One of O’Leary’s most significant achievements came through the software industry.

Together with his partners, he co-founded SoftKey Software Products, which later became known as The Learning Company.

Through strategic acquisitions and aggressive growth, the company evolved into one of the world's largest educational software businesses.

The eventual sale of the company for billions of dollars transformed O’Leary and his team into highly successful entrepreneurs.

Yet he often notes that the wealth itself was not the most important lesson.

The real lesson was understanding how businesses scale, create value, and solve problems for customers.

Saving, Investing, and Building Wealth

Much of O’Leary’s financial philosophy originated from lessons taught by his mother.

She emphasized living within one's means and avoiding unnecessary spending.

Today, he encourages people to:

  • Save at least 20% of their income
  • Invest consistently over decades
  • Avoid lifestyle inflation
  • Think long-term rather than short-term

He believes disciplined investing over 20 to 30 years can create substantial wealth even for individuals earning average incomes.

Small financial decisions repeated consistently often produce extraordinary results.

Making Your First $100,000

For individuals looking to increase their income, O’Leary strongly supports developing additional skills and side businesses.

He points to opportunities such as:

  • Video editing
  • Content creation
  • Social media management
  • Commercial production
  • Digital marketing
  • Creative freelance services

Rather than relying solely on traditional employment, he encourages people to monetize valuable skills and adapt to the modern economy.

According to O’Leary, today's opportunities are broader than ever for individuals willing to learn and execute.

Hiring Great People

O’Leary firmly believes that talent attracts talent.

One of the principles he follows throughout his companies is:

“Slow to hire. Quick to fire.”

He believes organizations perform best when every team member is aligned with the mission and committed to excellence.

At the same time, he emphasizes rewarding high performers and sharing success with the people who contribute to it.

Building a great business, he argues, is never a solo effort.

The Bigger Picture

Kevin O’Leary’s career demonstrates that lasting success is built through focus, discipline, continuous learning, and strategic decision-making.

While his achievements span industries ranging from media and software to investing and television, the principles behind his success remain remarkably consistent.

  • Focus on what matters most
  • Master your time
  • Build financial discipline
  • Invest in valuable skills
  • Surround yourself with exceptional people
  • Stay adaptable and keep learning

For O’Leary, wealth has never been the final destination. It is a tool that creates freedom, opportunities, and the ability to help others succeed.

His journey reminds entrepreneurs that true success is not measured solely by money, but by the freedom, impact, and choices that money makes possible.
doc: article/Kevin O’Leary_ Business tips from the Shark.docx

name: Laura Kaslow
title: Who Knew? Creating Ornaments from Maps Could Help Pay for Equine Therapy
story:
Who Knew? Creating Ornaments from Maps Could Help Pay for Equine Therapy

Some businesses are born from opportunity. Others are born from necessity. For Laura Kaslow, the creation of Maptometry emerged from a deeply personal mission: finding a way to support her daughter's health while pursuing her passion for art.

What began as a creative solution to a family challenge evolved into a unique artistic venture that combines history, storytelling, sustainability, and craftsmanship. Today, Maptometry stands as a testament to the power of creativity and the extraordinary things that can happen when purpose and passion intersect.

An Artist with a Mission

Laura Kaslow is many things—an artist, a mother, an entrepreneur, and a passionate advocate for creativity. Over the years, she has built a distinctive niche by transforming antique optometric lenses into stunning works of art using recycled vintage and antique maps.

Her creations are unlike anything else.

Each piece combines:

  • Authentic antique optometric lenses
  • Vintage and historical maps
  • Recycled materials
  • Handcrafted artistry
  • Personal stories and memories

But behind every ornament and pendant lies a story that goes far beyond artistic expression.

The Inspiration Behind Maptometry

The idea for Maptometry was inspired by Laura's desire to provide her daughter with access to equine therapy.

Her daughter was diagnosed with Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome, a connective tissue disorder that can significantly impact daily life and physical well-being.

Doctors recommended equine therapy as a beneficial treatment option. This specialized form of therapy uses interactions with horses to support physical, emotional, and occupational development.

For Laura's family, the therapy offered hope and meaningful progress.

There was just one challenge.

Insurance did not cover the cost.

Faced with the financial burden of ongoing therapy, Laura searched for a creative way to generate additional income while staying connected to her artistic talents.

What started as a solution to a family challenge would eventually become a thriving artistic business.

Turning Maps into Meaningful Art

Laura's breakthrough came when she began experimenting with antique optometric lenses once used by eye doctors.

Rather than allowing these unique historical objects to be discarded, she found a way to give them new life.

By carefully embedding authentic maps within the lenses, she created one-of-a-kind ornaments, pendants, and keepsakes that combined history, geography, and personal meaning.

Unlike many artists who rely on reproductions, Laura remains committed to authenticity.

“I use real maps. I don’t photocopy them. There is history, there is recycling.”

Each piece contains actual vintage cartography, preserving fragments of the past while transforming them into contemporary works of art.

Where Sustainability Meets Storytelling

One of the defining characteristics of Maptometry is its commitment to repurposing and sustainability.

Laura believes that discarded materials often possess untapped beauty and significance. Through her work, she demonstrates how forgotten objects can be reimagined and given a second life.

Her artistic process incorporates:

  • Recycled antique lenses
  • Vintage maps
  • Historical materials
  • Handcrafted assembly techniques

The result is artwork that celebrates both history and environmental responsibility.

For Laura, sustainability is not simply a business strategy—it is an extension of her values.

The Stories Hidden Inside Every Map

While the visual beauty of her creations attracts attention, Laura often finds that the most meaningful part of her work is the human connection it creates.

When customers browse her collection and select a map location, they frequently begin sharing personal stories connected to that place.

Some recall childhood memories.

Others remember family vacations, hometowns, loved ones, or important milestones.

What begins as a purchase often becomes a conversation.

Every map holds a story, and every customer brings a new chapter.

Through Maptometry, Laura has created more than artwork—she has created a bridge between personal memories and physical keepsakes.

Success Beyond Financial Rewards

Like many artists, Laura understands the challenges of building a sustainable business in the creative industry.

Yet her definition of success extends far beyond revenue.

One of the greatest rewards of her work is the opportunity to meet people and hear their stories.

Each interaction reinforces her belief that art has the power to connect individuals, spark conversations, and inspire new perspectives.

For Laura, those moments are just as valuable as any sale.

The Transformative Power of Art

At the heart of Laura's work is a deep belief in the importance of artistic expression.

She believes art encourages people to look at the world differently, broadening perspectives and creating opportunities for reflection and connection.

Whether someone is drawn to a piece because of its history, craftsmanship, or emotional significance, Laura sees art as a vehicle for discovery.

Art allows us to preserve memories, celebrate history, and find beauty in unexpected places.

The Bigger Picture

Laura Kaslow's journey demonstrates how creativity can emerge from life's most difficult circumstances.

What began as an effort to fund her daughter's therapy evolved into a distinctive business built on authenticity, sustainability, and storytelling.

Through Maptometry, she transformed antique lenses and forgotten maps into meaningful works of art while creating opportunities to support her family and connect with others.

  • Turning challenges into opportunities
  • Using creativity to solve real-world problems
  • Preserving history through art
  • Supporting sustainability through repurposing
  • Creating connections through storytelling

Today, every ornament and pendant Laura creates serves as a reminder that meaningful art often comes from deeply personal experiences.

Her story proves that when passion meets purpose, even the most unexpected ideas can become something truly extraordinary.

name: Philip Reynolds
title: Embracing Authenticity: Philip Reynolds’ Journey of Compassion and Resilience in Mental Health Care
story:
Embracing Authenticity: Philip Reynolds’ Journey of Compassion and Resilience in Mental Health Care

In a profession where empathy, trust, and human connection can profoundly impact lives, Philip Reynolds has dedicated nearly two decades to helping individuals navigate some of their most challenging moments. As a licensed clinical social worker, therapist, executive leader, and coach, Philip has built a career rooted in compassion, authenticity, and a genuine commitment to serving others.

His story is not simply about professional success—it is about creating meaningful connections, empowering people to heal, and remaining true to oneself in an increasingly complex world.

A Calling Rooted in Service

Philip Reynolds entered the field of behavioral and mental health with a desire to make a meaningful difference in people's lives. Armed with a Master of Social Work degree and LCSW-C certification, he began building a career focused on helping individuals overcome challenges and achieve lasting growth.

His professional journey started at a counseling and learning center in Watts, California, before taking him through various roles across West Los Angeles and eventually to the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area.

Over the years, he worked in:

  • Counseling and learning centers
  • Residential treatment programs
  • Private practice settings
  • Mental health leadership positions
  • Executive coaching environments

Each experience expanded his understanding of human behavior, resilience, and the importance of individualized care.

Adapting During Uncertain Times

Like many professionals, Philip faced significant changes during the global pandemic.

Rather than viewing disruption as a setback, he embraced it as an opportunity to serve more people in new ways.

During this period, he helped launch Partial Hospitalization Programs (PHP) and Intensive Outpatient Programs (IOP) throughout the Virginia region while continuing his private practice and executive coaching work.

His ability to adapt reflected a philosophy that has guided much of his career:

Growth often requires flexibility, resilience, and a willingness to embrace change.

By responding proactively to evolving mental health needs, Philip was able to expand his impact while helping organizations navigate unprecedented challenges.

Leading with Compassion

Today, Philip serves both as a private practitioner and a senior leader within behavioral health organizations.

His responsibilities extend beyond therapy sessions and clinical work. He also oversees operations, supports teams, and helps ensure that individuals receive high-quality mental health care across multiple locations.

Despite the administrative demands of leadership, his focus remains consistent:

People always come first.

Whether working directly with clients or mentoring professionals within his organization, Philip believes genuine care is one of the most powerful tools available in mental health care.

The Importance of Human Connection

At the center of Philip's approach is the belief that every person wants to feel seen, heard, and valued.

For him, therapy is not simply about techniques or treatment plans. It begins with building trust.

He understands that meaningful healing often occurs when individuals feel safe enough to share their experiences openly and honestly.

This emphasis on connection extends beyond clients.

Philip also prioritizes creating strong relationships with colleagues, leaders, and community partners.

By fostering collaboration and mutual respect, he helps create environments where both professionals and clients can thrive.

Navigating Challenges with Perspective

Working in mental health inevitably brings challenges.

Complex cases, organizational demands, and rapidly changing circumstances require constant adaptation.

Rather than resisting obstacles, Philip views them as a natural part of growth.

Challenges are not signs of failure—they are often indicators of progress.

To maintain perspective, he intentionally surrounds himself with advisors, mentors, and collaborators who bring different experiences and viewpoints.

This diversity of thought helps him make better decisions while continuing to grow personally and professionally.

The Courage to Be Authentic

One of the defining themes throughout Philip's journey is authenticity.

In a world often shaped by expectations, comparisons, and external pressures, he remains committed to showing up as his genuine self.

His faith, values, and personal principles serve as anchors that guide his decisions and interactions.

Rather than seeking approval from everyone around him, he focuses on living in alignment with what matters most.

Authenticity is not about being perfect. It is about being honest about who you are.

That authenticity has become one of his greatest strengths, allowing him to build deeper relationships and foster trust with the people he serves.

The Role of Vulnerability in Growth

Philip believes meaningful growth often begins with vulnerability.

Many people spend years hiding struggles, fears, or insecurities because they worry about judgment.

Through his work, he encourages open dialogue and honest communication.

By creating space for vulnerability, individuals can:

  • Develop stronger self-awareness
  • Build healthier relationships
  • Process difficult experiences
  • Increase emotional resilience
  • Create meaningful personal growth

For Philip, vulnerability is not weakness—it is a pathway to healing.

Finding Strength Through Humor

One characteristic that consistently appears throughout Philip's personal and professional life is his sense of humor.

While mental health work often involves serious topics, he believes laughter plays an important role in resilience.

Humor helps people:

  • Reduce stress
  • Build connection
  • Navigate difficult situations
  • Maintain perspective
  • Find hope during challenging times

Philip often describes humor as one of life's most powerful coping mechanisms.

Laughter does not eliminate challenges, but it can make them easier to carry.

His ability to balance professionalism with warmth and humor helps create a welcoming environment for both clients and colleagues.

The Most Rewarding Part of the Journey

For Philip, success is not measured by titles, positions, or accomplishments.

The most meaningful moments come when former clients reach out years later to share how their lives have changed.

Hearing stories of personal growth, healing, and transformation reminds him why he chose this profession in the first place.

Every message of gratitude reinforces the profound impact that compassion and connection can have on someone's life.

Being trusted with another person's journey is both a privilege and a responsibility.

The Bigger Picture

Philip Reynolds' story is a powerful reminder that leadership and healing begin with authenticity.

Throughout nearly two decades in mental health care, he has remained committed to helping others while staying true to his own values.

His journey reflects the importance of:

  • Compassion
  • Authenticity
  • Resilience
  • Human connection
  • Continuous growth
  • Leading with empathy

Whether supporting clients through personal challenges, mentoring professionals, or leading mental health organizations, Philip continues to demonstrate that genuine care can transform lives.

As he looks toward the future, his mission remains unchanged: creating spaces where people feel understood, supported, and empowered to become the best versions of themselves.

His story reminds us that sometimes the greatest impact comes not from having all the answers, but from showing up with empathy, authenticity, and a willingness to walk alongside others on their journey.

name: Stephen Sindoni
title: Key to Success: Steering an Enterprise and Life Through a Military Mindset
story:
Key to Success: Steering an Enterprise and Life Through a Military Mindset

How does an entrepreneur, retired military officer, engineer, martial arts instructor, and father successfully balance career, business, family, and personal growth—all while maintaining a positive outlook?

For Stephen Sindoni, the answer lies in the values he developed during his years of military service: discipline, perseverance, integrity, and leadership.

After traveling extensively across the United States and around the world, Sindoni has gained invaluable lessons that continue to shape both his professional and personal life. Today, he applies those lessons as a systems sales engineer, martial arts school owner, mentor, and community leader.

From Military Service to Entrepreneurship

Following an honorable career in the United States Navy, Stephen transitioned into civilian life while continuing to pursue meaningful work.

He currently serves as a Systems Sales Engineer at Cubic Mission & Performance Solutions while also operating USSD Irvine 2, a martial arts studio under the umbrella of United Studios of Self Defense (USSD).

USSD is one of North America's largest martial arts organizations, with a history spanning more than six decades. The organization is dedicated to teaching self-defense, discipline, confidence, and personal growth to students of all ages.

At Sindoni's studio, students range from preschool-aged children to adults in their sixties and beyond, all united by a desire to improve themselves physically and mentally.

The Long Journey to Black Belt

One of the most valuable lessons martial arts teaches is patience.

Students begin as White Belts and progress through multiple levels, including:

  • Yellow Belt
  • Orange Belt
  • Purple Belt
  • Blue Belt
  • Green Belt
  • Brown Belt Degrees
  • First Degree Black Belt

Unlike many modern pursuits that promise quick results, earning a First Degree Black Belt often requires 10 to 12 years of consistent effort and dedication.

“Nothing worthwhile comes easy. Everything requires work, patience, and perseverance.”

This philosophy mirrors many of the lessons Sindoni learned during his military career.

More Than Self-Defense

While many people initially join martial arts to learn self-defense, the benefits extend far beyond physical techniques.

Students develop:

  • Discipline
  • Confidence
  • Focus
  • Physical fitness
  • Strategic thinking
  • Emotional resilience

Through sparring, students learn how to remain calm under pressure while improving reaction time and decision-making.

Forms training helps students better understand movement, balance, coordination, and personal strengths.

Meditation and mindfulness practices further strengthen mental clarity and emotional control.

Martial arts is as much about mastering the mind as it is about mastering the body.

The Military Influence

Years of military service taught Sindoni that success is built through consistency and commitment.

He firmly believes that character matters just as much as skill.

“We all have differences, but we have even more similarities.”

Throughout his career, he has worked alongside people from diverse cultures, backgrounds, and experiences. These interactions reinforced his belief that integrity, teamwork, and mutual respect are essential to achieving meaningful goals.

Whether in the military, business, or martial arts, he emphasizes personal accountability and ownership.

People who take responsibility for their circumstances, he believes, are better positioned to improve them.

Building Leaders Through STORM

One of the programs Sindoni is particularly proud of is the Specialized Team of Role Models (STORM).

STORM is designed to help students develop leadership skills through teaching, mentoring, and teamwork.

Participants learn that teaching a skill requires a deeper understanding than simply performing it.

“It's different to be taught something than it is to teach it.”

By explaining techniques to younger students and assisting instructors, participants reinforce their own knowledge while building confidence and communication skills.

The program also introduces students to leadership, responsibility, teamwork, and charitable service—skills that extend far beyond the dojo.

A Personal Motivation

Sindoni's passion for martial arts began long before he opened his studio.

As a child, he enjoyed martial arts movies and dreamed of training someday. However, opportunities were limited.

After serving in the military and retiring from active duty in 2015, he decided to pursue martial arts seriously while helping his daughter continue her own training.

What started as a way to stay physically active quickly became something much bigger.

Eventually, he opened his own studio with the goal of providing opportunities for others that he never had growing up.

His daughter would go on to earn her First Degree Black Belt at just fourteen years old—a moment of immense pride for the entire family.

The Value of Persistence

One lesson Sindoni frequently teaches is the importance of seeing things through.

“It's easy to walk away from difficult things. If quitting becomes your first option, it will always be your exit strategy.”

Whether learning martial arts, building a business, or pursuing personal goals, meaningful achievements require persistence.
