Tasks that initially seem difficult often become manageable through repetition, effort, and commitment.

Learning Through Competition

Twice each year, USSD students participate in regional tournaments.

These events allow students to compete in:

  • Forms
  • Open Forms
  • Weapons Demonstrations
  • Sparring

Beyond competition, tournaments provide opportunities for students to:

  • Meet peers from other schools
  • Learn from different instructors
  • Build confidence
  • Gain valuable experience
  • Develop sportsmanship

Winning is never the sole objective.

Growth, learning, and personal development remain the primary goals.

A Global Perspective

Throughout his military career, Sindoni traveled extensively, including assignments and visits to:

  • Germany
  • Japan
  • Iraq
  • Afghanistan
  • China
  • Taiwan
  • South Korea
  • Numerous European countries

Despite cultural differences, he observed a common theme everywhere he went.

People want meaningful relationships, purpose, and opportunities to use their talents.

These experiences strengthened his commitment to creating an environment where students feel valued, respected, and included.

Creating a Sense of Belonging

One of Sindoni's primary goals is to ensure that every student feels part of something larger than themselves.

He strives to create a culture that feels like family—one where individuals can share ideas, learn from one another, and grow together.

He also believes strongly in helping young people develop their own voices and confidence.

Whether male or female, every student should learn to trust themselves, understand their values, and develop independence.

Leadership begins when individuals learn who they are and what they stand for.

Navigating Change and Uncertainty

A few years ago, Sindoni's studio experienced significant organizational changes involving management, staff, and students.

Like many business owners, he found himself navigating uncertainty while trying to maintain stability.

The experience taught him several important lessons:

  • Business is fragile
  • Support systems matter
  • Adaptability is critical
  • Strong relationships create resilience

Throughout the transition, he relied heavily on the support of family, friends, fellow studio owners, instructors, students, and parents.

“I couldn't have done it alone.”

Although the challenges delayed expansion plans, they ultimately strengthened both his leadership and his business.

Running a Business with Purpose

Operating a martial arts studio in a competitive market requires constant attention to detail.

Sindoni focuses on maintaining a facility that is:

  • Professional
  • Safe
  • Clean
  • Welcoming
  • Focused on student growth

He understands that parents entrust him with their children's development, making consistency and professionalism non-negotiable.

The Bigger Picture

Stephen Sindoni's journey demonstrates how military principles can successfully translate into entrepreneurship, leadership, and personal development.

Whether mentoring students, leading a business, or supporting his community, he remains guided by values that have shaped his entire life:

  • Discipline
  • Integrity
  • Perseverance
  • Service
  • Leadership
  • Continuous improvement

His message is simple but powerful:

“Life is made up of small actions repeated every day. If we consistently do the little things well, we eventually accomplish great things.”

Through martial arts, mentorship, and leadership, Stephen Sindoni continues helping others build confidence, character, and the resilience necessary to achieve their goals—both inside and outside the dojo.

name: Terry Hairston
title: Transforming Lives Through Fitness, Recovery, and Purpose
story:
Transforming Lives Through Fitness, Recovery, and Purpose

In a world often driven by self-interest, Terry Hairston has dedicated his life to serving others. From becoming a successful professional bodybuilder to founding an organization that helps individuals overcome addiction, his journey is a powerful story of resilience, redemption, and purpose.

Terry's life demonstrates that adversity does not define a person. Instead, it is how we respond to adversity that shapes our future.

Growing Up in Challenging Circumstances

Terry's early years were marked by the harsh realities of alcoholism, substance abuse, and the challenges that often accompany those environments.

Like many young people growing up in difficult circumstances, he found himself surrounded by negative influences and destructive paths.

At a critical moment in his life, Terry made a decision that would alter his future forever.

He chose to break the cycle instead of becoming part of it.

Determined to create a different future, he walked away from a lifestyle centered around selling drugs and embraced a path focused on health, discipline, and personal growth.

That decision became the foundation of everything that followed.

Finding Strength Through Fitness

Fitness entered Terry's life as more than a physical activity—it became a vehicle for transformation.

Bodybuilding taught him lessons that extended far beyond the gym:

  • Discipline
  • Consistency
  • Mental toughness
  • Self-respect
  • Personal accountability

As he dedicated himself to training, he discovered that physical strength often develops alongside emotional and mental resilience.

Through years of commitment and hard work, Terry achieved success as a professional bodybuilder. Yet the trophies and accomplishments were only part of the story.

Fitness had given him something even more valuable—a sense of purpose.

A Personal Loss That Changed Everything

While Terry's success in fitness was growing, life presented him with a devastating challenge.

The loss of his brother to alcoholism became one of the most painful moments of his life.

Rather than allowing grief to consume him, Terry transformed that pain into a mission.

His brother's memory became the driving force behind a movement dedicated to helping others reclaim their lives.

Determined to create lasting change, Terry established The Tracy Dion Hairston Foundation, named in honor of his late brother.

The foundation was created with a simple but powerful goal:

To help individuals struggling with addiction find recovery, healing, and hope.

Building a Foundation for Recovery

The Tracy Dion Hairston Foundation serves as a support system for individuals seeking a healthier future.

Its mission extends beyond sobriety.

The organization focuses on helping people rebuild their lives through:

  • Fitness and wellness programs
  • Mentorship and coaching
  • Community support
  • Personal development
  • Recovery resources

Terry understands that addiction affects far more than physical health. It impacts relationships, self-worth, mental health, and one's overall sense of identity.

Because of this, his approach to recovery addresses the entire individual rather than focusing solely on addiction itself.

Healing From the Inside Out

One of the core principles guiding Terry's work is the belief that lasting transformation begins internally.

Many individuals battling addiction are also carrying emotional wounds, trauma, unresolved pain, or deeply rooted struggles that have never been addressed.

Simply removing substances from someone's life is often not enough.

True recovery begins when people start healing the pain beneath the addiction.

Through mentorship, counseling, structured fitness programs, and ongoing support, Terry helps individuals develop the tools necessary to rebuild confidence, resilience, and self-belief.

His goal is not simply to help people survive addiction but to help them create lives they are excited to live.

A Holistic Approach to Transformation

What distinguishes Terry's foundation is its holistic philosophy.

Rather than viewing addiction as purely a physical dependency, he recognizes it as a complex challenge involving multiple factors:

  • Psychological struggles
  • Emotional trauma
  • Environmental influences
  • Social circumstances
  • Physical health

To address these interconnected challenges, the foundation combines multiple avenues of support.

Fitness becomes a tool for building confidence.

Mentorship provides guidance.

Community creates belonging.

Recovery programs offer accountability.

Together, these elements create an environment where individuals can rediscover their potential.

Leading By Example

Beyond his foundation work, Terry is also a successful business owner and operator of multiple fitness centers.

His leadership philosophy can be summarized in three words:

“Lead By Example.”

Rather than simply telling others what is possible, Terry demonstrates it through his own actions.

His personal journey—from adversity to achievement—serves as living proof that meaningful change is possible.

Clients and community members are inspired not only by what he teaches but by what he embodies every day.

For Terry, leadership is rooted in:

  • Integrity
  • Empathy
  • Accountability
  • Compassion
  • Consistency

These values guide every aspect of his work and influence the lives of those he serves.

Creating Lasting Impact

Terry's mission extends far beyond fitness centers and recovery programs.

His vision is to create a culture where individuals understand that their circumstances do not determine their future.

Every person has the capacity to grow, heal, and transform when given the proper support and opportunity.

Through education, mentorship, and community outreach, Terry continues expanding the reach of his foundation and inspiring people to believe in themselves again.

The Bigger Picture

Terry Hairston's story is ultimately about more than bodybuilding, business, or even recovery.

It is a story about turning pain into purpose.

It is about using personal struggles to help others overcome theirs.

It is about choosing service over self-interest and hope over despair.

His journey reminds us that transformation is always possible, regardless of where we begin.

When compassion meets determination, lives change.

Through his work, Terry continues proving that resilience, faith, and genuine care for others can create lasting impact—not only for individuals but for entire communities.

In a world often defined by challenges and division, Terry Hairston stands as a powerful reminder that one person can make a difference, and that every act of service has the potential to transform a life.

name: Catherine Hahn
title: Crafting Careers, Transforming Lives, and Building Communities
author: Jayanthi Krishna
story:
Crafting Careers, Transforming Lives, and Building Communities

For Catherine Hahn, a career is far more than a job title or a paycheck. It is a reflection of purpose, passion, and personal fulfillment. As a career coach, personal brand strategist, mentor, and entrepreneur, Hahn has dedicated her life to helping people discover meaningful career paths and build lives aligned with their goals.

Today, she coaches everyone from high school students and college graduates to executives and CEOs, guiding them through career transitions, personal branding, leadership development, and long-term life planning.

“Everyone has a passion. My goal is to help people identify it, nurture it, and turn it into a fulfilling career.”

An Unconventional Career Journey

Catherine's own career path was anything but linear.

After graduating from UCLA with a passion for history and classical civilization, she accepted an internship as a curator at the Getty Museum. While she enjoyed the subject matter, she quickly realized that the role did not provide the fulfillment she was seeking.

Her next opportunity came as an executive administrative assistant. However, the position ended abruptly when she was let go during her very first week.

Although disappointing, that setback became a turning point.

Determined to move forward, Hahn explored opportunities online—an emerging resource at the time—and secured a position in sales at Mason Concepts.

There, she learned one of the most valuable lessons of her career:

Preparation creates opportunity.

That lesson would continue to shape her professional growth for years to come.

Building Expertise Through Recruiting

Hahn later entered the recruiting industry, working for an IT staffing company. When the company dissolved, she joined one of its former business owners and began specializing in government recruiting for the City of Los Angeles.

At only twenty-two years old, she embraced every opportunity that came her way.

Over time, she recruited across numerous industries, including:

  • Healthcare
  • Legal Services
  • Marketing
  • Technology
  • Government Agencies

Rather than limiting herself to one specialty, Hahn continuously expanded her knowledge and experience.

“Say yes to opportunities. You never know where they might lead.”

Discovering the Power of Resume Writing

While working in recruitment, Hahn noticed a common pattern.

Many qualified candidates struggled to communicate their value effectively through their resumes.

She realized that a resume was more than a document—it was a strategic tool that connected candidates to opportunities.

Drawing upon her strong writing background, she began helping job seekers improve their resumes and position themselves more effectively in the marketplace.

What started as a side service soon evolved into a specialized profession.

As her family responsibilities grew and remote work became increasingly important, Hahn launched her own company:

Affordable Resume Coach.

As a certified resume writer and interview professional, she expanded her services to include career coaching, personal branding, interview preparation, and strategic career planning.

Turning Family Challenges into Opportunities

The year 2020 brought new challenges and unexpected opportunities.

As her parents prepared to retire from their real estate business, Hahn and her younger brother stepped in to continue managing the company.

At the same time, her teenage daughters struggled with the emotional impact of pandemic lockdowns.

Searching for a meaningful project, they came up with an idea inspired by a news story:

What if they created a small community library to encourage reading?

With Hahn's support and mentorship, they launched what would become the Dynamite Book Club.

Building a Community Through Books

The project started modestly, using books from their personal collection.

Over time, neighbors and community members began showing interest.

People donated books.

Volunteers joined.

New book boxes appeared throughout the community.

What began as a simple family project evolved into something much larger.

Today, the initiative includes:

  • 15 community libraries
  • Several additional locations in development
  • Student volunteers
  • Community outreach programs

The project provides young people with leadership opportunities while promoting literacy and community engagement.

“I want young people to discover purpose through service.”

The Power of Mentorship

Beyond career coaching, mentorship has become one of Hahn's greatest passions.

She regularly helps students create meaningful projects, gain volunteer experience, and develop practical leadership skills.

Her philosophy is simple:

“Don't fear asking. The worst someone can say is no.”

She encourages students to take risks, explore opportunities, and develop confidence through action rather than waiting for perfect circumstances.

Overcoming Fear and Social Anxiety

One reason Hahn connects so deeply with young people is that she understands their fears firsthand.

As a child and teenager, she struggled significantly with social anxiety.

Simple social situations often felt overwhelming, and she frequently avoided large gatherings whenever possible.

Today, she openly shares those experiences to help others understand that confidence is a skill that can be developed.

Among her practical advice:

  • Focus on your message rather than your audience.
  • Practice repeatedly before important events.
  • Create action plans for intimidating situations.
  • Develop a confident mindset through preparation.

She often discusses the concept of narrow focus—the ability to concentrate on what matters while ignoring distractions and fears.

“If you say ‘I can’ and ‘yes,’ you open doors you never imagined existed.”

Learning from Failure

Failure is not something Hahn fears.

In fact, she considers it a necessary part of growth.

When discussing setbacks, she often references Aaliyah's famous lyric:

“Dust yourself off and try again.”

For Hahn, resilience is not about avoiding failure but learning how to recover from it.

Dwelling on mistakes only creates barriers. Progress happens when people choose to move forward.

Helping Students Think Long-Term

One challenge Hahn frequently encounters is that many students underestimate how quickly time passes.

She encourages them to imagine themselves ten years into the future and begin planning accordingly.

Questions she often asks include:

  • What lifestyle do you want?
  • What kind of work excites you?
  • What goals do you hope to achieve?
  • What actions can you take today?

Her emphasis on planning, discipline, and time management helps students create realistic roadmaps toward their desired futures.

One of her favorite tools is the vision board.

“If you can visualize your future, you can begin building it.”

Career Coaching Beyond Job Placement

While many people associate career coaching solely with finding employment, Hahn's services extend much further.

She helps clients navigate:

  • Career changes
  • Leadership development
  • Personal branding
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Business development
  • Retirement lifestyle planning
  • Long-term strategic growth

Rather than viewing retirement as the end of work, she views it as a lifestyle design process.

Her focus is helping clients create careers and lives that support their personal goals and aspirations.

Staying Organized While Wearing Many Hats

Balancing multiple responsibilities requires discipline and organization.

As a career coach, mentor, entrepreneur, property manager, caregiver, and community leader, Hahn relies heavily on planning systems to stay focused.

In recent years, she has adopted a detailed planner and gratitude journal to manage her responsibilities and maintain perspective.

She jokingly credits her ADHD for her seemingly endless energy.

“Writing things down changed everything for me.”

Giving Back Through Volunteerism

Community service remains one of Hahn's core values.

She actively encourages students to volunteer, gain hands-on experience, and contribute to causes larger than themselves.

According to Hahn, volunteering offers benefits beyond professional development:

  • Improved mental health
  • Expanded perspectives
  • Leadership experience
  • Networking opportunities
  • Personal fulfillment

She believes these experiences help shape stronger, more compassionate future leaders.

Finding Balance Through Hobbies

Despite her busy schedule, Hahn strongly believes everyone needs hobbies and personal interests outside of work.

One of her favorite activities is metal detecting along local beaches.

Among her discoveries is a silver ring she proudly considers one of her most memorable finds.

For Hahn, hobbies serve as an important reminder that productivity and personal well-being must coexist.

The Bigger Picture

Catherine Hahn's story is ultimately about empowerment.

Whether helping someone write a resume, launch a career, build a community project, overcome fear, or discover a new direction in life, her mission remains the same:

Help people realize their potential and create lives filled with purpose.

Through career coaching, mentorship, community leadership, and entrepreneurship, she continues transforming lives one conversation, one opportunity, and one success story at a time.

Her message is simple yet powerful:

Say yes to opportunities. Learn from failure. Keep moving forward. The possibilities are far greater than you think.
